notes: Tested whether the engineering layer contradicts, weakens, or inverts the traditional meaning. (1) Does it contradict the kṣatriya-specificity? No — it preserves it as role-specific accumulated context, narrowing rather than generalising. (2) Does it weaken the 'na vidyate' (none other exists) claim? No — it renders it as 'there is no nobler escape into a cleaner problem' which holds the absolute. (3) Does it invert the duty-argument? No — it preserves duty-as-binding-because-of-role-context, which is what svadharma names. (4) Does it import a connotation the Sanskrit lacks? It uses 'imposter-recoil' as a frame in the iteration log but not in the verse itself; the verse stays in scope. Reverse-translate test: if a Sanskrit reader reads only the engineering layer, would they reconstruct a stance compatible with svadharma + api + dharmya yuddha? Yes — the role-bound-context, the second-independent-argument framing, and the role's-highest-good are all reconstructable. PASS on both 3.6 and 7.10.
